name: dispersal_sloughing
title: Dispersal - light-cell sloughing
question: Does a negative light-Medium tension (gamma_lM=-5) make light cells slough off and disperse into the medium?
hypothesis: Light cells prefer medium contact and detach as isolated cells while dark cells stay compact and round.
objective: Simulate gamma_lM<0 and observe light-cell dispersal into the medium at 480 MCS (unannealed).
description: Reproduction of the Fig 25+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=14, J_dd=4, J_ld=11, J_lM=2, J_dM=16 (gamma_ld=+2, gamma_lM=-5, gamma_dM=+14); T=5.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_random. Figures reproduced: Fig 25.
claim: Light cells disperse as isolated cells into the medium while dark cells remain compact and round (Fig 25).
expected_behavior: Light cells detach from the aggregate into the medium
Dark cells remain a compact rounded mass

findings.statement: Light cells disperse as isolated cells into the medium while dark cells remain compact and round (Fig 25).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Dispersal - light-cell sloughing example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 25.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

derived.insight: Light cells disperse as isolated cells into the medium while dark cells remain compact and round (Fig 25).
